title: MrBeast | Jimmy Donaldson, YouTube, Age, Bio 2026
subtitle: The most-subscribed solo creator on YouTube, with 350M+ subscribers, a $5M game show on Amazon Prime, and a growing consumer brand empire
content_html:
Who Is MrBeast | Jimmy Donaldson Profile

MrBeast is the online alias of Jimmy Donaldson, an American YouTuber, entrepreneur, and philanthropist born on May 7, 1998, in Wichita, Kansas, and raised in Greenville, North Carolina. He is the most-subscribed solo creator on YouTube, surpassing 350 million subscribers on his main channel by early 2026, a milestone that no individual creator had reached before.

Donaldson launched his YouTube channel in 2012 at age 13, initially posting gaming content and commentary. His channel gained traction in 2017 after viral videos counting numbers and reading dictionaries demonstrated extreme persistence. By 2018, he had established his signature format: high-production, high-stakes challenge and stunt videos where large cash prizes, acts of mass generosity, or both, serve as the central hook.

As of 2026, MrBeast operates one of the largest media and consumer brand ecosystems built from a single YouTube channel. His work spans multiple YouTube channels, a chocolate bar company, a virtual restaurant chain, and a global game show produced for Amazon Prime Video. He is frequently cited as the defining example of a new generation of creator-entrepreneur.

MrBeast YouTube Analytics 2026 | Channel Data

MrBeast's YouTube presence spans four primary channels. The main @MrBeast channel is the anchor, but gaming, philanthropy, and archive content extend his total reach significantly. Below is the channel-by-channel breakdown as tracked in early 2026.

Main Channel: @MrBeast

Metric Value (Early 2026)
Subscribers350M+
Total Views65B+
Videos Published800+
Avg. Views Per Video (2024-25)80M - 150M
Channel LaunchFebruary 20, 2012
Upload Frequency~2 videos/month
Most Viewed Video"I Spent 50 Hours Buried Alive" (350M+ views)

Additional Channels

Channel Subscribers Focus
@MrBeastGaming51M+Gaming challenge content
@Beast Philanthropy31M+Charity, food banks, disaster relief
@MrBeast218M+Behind-the-scenes, extras, vlogs

Donaldson crossed 100 million subscribers in 2022, 200 million in 2023, and 300 million in early 2024, making him the fastest solo creator to reach each consecutive milestone. By comparison, the T-Series channel, which is owned by an Indian music label rather than an individual, holds higher subscriber counts but does not represent a single person. MrBeast is the most-subscribed individual human on the platform.

His upload cadence is deliberately low by YouTube standards. Where high-frequency creators post multiple times per week, Donaldson typically publishes twice per month on the main channel. The trade-off is production scale: single videos regularly cost between $1 million and $10 million to produce, with one shoot for his island-survival challenge reportedly exceeding $14 million in total costs. This investment directly drives view counts that dwarf most other creators.

Beast Games | Amazon Prime Video Season 1

Beast Games premiered on Amazon Prime Video on December 19, 2024, as the largest game show in television history by number of contestants. The show featured 1,000 players competing in a series of increasingly challenging elimination rounds for a single prize of $5 million, the largest cash prize in game show history.

The show became one of Amazon Prime Video's most-watched originals at launch, drawing significant media attention both for its scale and for allegations of mistreatment made by some contestants during production. Donaldson co-created and hosted the show, producing it through his media company alongside Amazon MGM Studios.

We wanted to make the biggest, most ambitious game show ever made. The logistics alone took 18 months of planning before we could shoot a single frame.

Season 2 casting was announced in early 2026. OzoneNews covered the casting details at launch. For full casting information, see Beast Games Season 2 | Casting .

MrBeast Brand Empire | Feastables, Burger, Media

Outside YouTube, Donaldson has built a consumer brand portfolio that leverages his audience reach. As of 2026, the two primary brands are Feastables and MrBeast Burger.

Feastables

Feastables is a direct-to-consumer chocolate bar brand founded in 2022. The brand is sold at Walmart, Target, and international retail chains, and was designed as a higher-margin alternative to the donated snacks that appeared in early MrBeast videos. Feastables generated an estimated $10 million in revenue in its first month of launch, driven almost entirely by Donaldson's YouTube audience.

The brand has expanded to include candy bars, cereal, and novelty items. Feastables positions itself as a better-ingredient option relative to mass-market chocolate, with no artificial colors and fewer preservatives. It competes directly with Mars and Nestle in the impulse-buy confectionery category despite having a fraction of the distribution budget, relying instead on creator-driven demand.

MrBeast Burger

MrBeast Burger launched in December 2020 as a virtual restaurant chain operating exclusively through ghost kitchens, third-party delivery apps, and partner restaurant locations. At its peak, the chain operated through over 1,700 locations across the United States. The model attracted criticism from partner restaurants who alleged poor food quality control and brand management inconsistencies. Donaldson publicly acknowledged quality issues and indicated plans to restructure the brand's operational model in 2023.

Beast Philanthropy | Community Impact

Philanthropy is a structural part of Donaldson's brand rather than a side project. The @Beast Philanthropy channel, managed by his team, documents large-scale charitable projects including food bank donations, disaster relief shipments, and community infrastructure projects.

Across documented campaigns, Beast Philanthropy has distributed over 100 million pounds of food to food banks across the United States, funded eye surgeries for over 1,000 people with curable blindness, built and donated over 100 homes internationally, and provided clean water infrastructure in multiple countries in sub-Saharan Africa and Latin America. The channel's content is produced separately from the main MrBeast challenge videos and operates with 100% of revenue directed back to charitable programs.

The scale of these projects, and the public documentation of costs in video descriptions and financial disclosures, has made the Beast Philanthropy model a reference case in discussions about creator-funded charity versus traditional nonprofit structures.

MrBeast Controversy | Beast Games Allegations, Lawsuits

Donaldson and his production company have faced several controversies, the most significant arising from the production of Beast Games Season 1 in 2024.

Beast Games Season 1 | Contestant Allegations

Following the December 2024 premiere of Beast Games on Amazon Prime Video, a group of former contestants filed a lawsuit in January 2025 alleging that production conditions during filming were unsafe and that promised accommodations and compensation were not delivered as agreed. The complaint specifically named:

  • Inadequate food and water provided to contestants during multi-day filming stretches
  • Failure to accommodate disclosed medical conditions and dietary restrictions
  • Retaliatory treatment of contestants who raised complaints during production
  • Alleged failure to pay agreed appearance fees to eliminated contestants

Donaldson's team denied the allegations in a statement provided to media outlets, calling the claims "grossly exaggerated" and noting that a production of 1,000 people was logistically unprecedented. The lawsuit was ongoing as of early 2026. Amazon MGM Studios did not directly comment on the production claims beyond confirming the show met its contractual obligations under the studio's production agreement.

Internal Culture Allegations (2023)

In mid-2023, several anonymous accounts on social media alleged toxic workplace dynamics within the MrBeast production team, including reports of a high-pressure environment, irregular payment, and favoritism. Donaldson addressed these allegations in a public video, acknowledging that the company had grown faster than its internal HR infrastructure and committing to structural changes. No formal legal claims were filed from this period.

Ava Kris Tyson Departure (2024)

In July 2024, longtime MrBeast collaborator Ava Kris Tyson departed from the channel following unrelated personal allegations circulated on social media. Donaldson confirmed her departure in a brief statement and did not elaborate publicly. The matter was not connected to the Beast Games lawsuit.

MrBeast Social Media | Official Accounts 2026

MrBeast maintains verified accounts across all major platforms. Official accounts as of April 2026:

  • YouTube: @MrBeast - 350M+ subscribers, main challenge and stunt videos
  • YouTube: @MrBeastGaming - 51M+ subscribers, gaming challenges
  • YouTube: @Beast Philanthropy - 31M+ subscribers, charitable campaigns
  • Instagram: @mrbeast - 55M+ followers
  • TikTok: @mrbeast - 112M+ followers
  • X (Twitter): @MrBeast - 35M+ followers
